overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on research related to information operations, computational social science, and disinformation. LinkedIn boasts the largest following, but engagement is distributed across all platforms. Recent activity indicates a focus on disseminating research findings, announcing funding opportunities, and seeking new team members.

contentThemes.description: Posts detailing new publications, research grants, and project updates.
Content related to computational methods for social media analysis and disinformation detection.
Posts advertising postdoctoral positions and research opportunities.
Content focused on the study and detection of disinformation campaigns and coordinated inauthentic behavior.

recommendations: Cross-promote content across platforms to maximize reach and engagement.
Utilize platform-specific features (e.g., LinkedIn articles, Bluesky collections) to enhance content presentation.
Engage directly with comments and replies to foster a sense of community.
Experiment with different content formats (e.g., videos, infographics) to attract attention.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky demonstrates a strong interest in research opportunities and publications related to coordinated inauthentic behavior, indicated by high repost rates. Leverage this by sharing more content of this nature.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon followers engage with posts about tool releases and methodological discussions related to disinformation. Continue sharing updates on CooRnet and similar projects.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn followers are most engaged with announcements of research grants and job opportunities. Maintain a professional tone and focus on career-related content.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
